notes: Aberdeen planned benchmark scope: all size companies in Global 5000 across all industries.

metric_value: 50-67% of contracts sub-optimal; only 5-10% fully optimal
notes: Prediction to be validated by full benchmark report due June 2006.

Gartner reported in 2003 that 50% of IT outsourcing deals in 2003 would be considered unsuccessful in delivering expected value. A 2005 Gartner study on customer service outsourcing predicted 80% of cost-motivated outsourcing deals would fail. A study of 30 ITO contracts found 40% failure rate. Dun & Bradstreet's Barometer of Global Outsourcing found 20-25% of outsourcing relationships fail within 2 years, 50% within 5. Only 5% of organizations in a worldwide survey by PA Consulting Group achieved expected cost savings from outsourcing. These findings broadly confirm predictions that IT outsourcing sub-optimal rates are substantial — in the range of 40-70%.
